title: About the consumer price surveys | Stats NZ
content:
## About the consumer price surveys
21 September 2023, 10:45am
Information about taking part in consumer price surveys, such as why we carry out the surveys, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
We carry out these surveys to collect information for the consumers price index (CPI). The CPI is a measure of price change of goods and services purchased by private New Zealand households; and inflation.
Consumer price surveys include:
- Retail outlets (prices of goods collected in stores)
- Postal surveys (all other consumer price surveys — eg dental services, construction, lawnmowing).
All businesses that supply goods and services to private households in New Zealand can be selected to participate in consumer price surveys.
- 2,800 retail outlets may be selected from the 12 pricing centres throughout the country: Whangarei, Auckland, Hamilton, Tauranga, Napier-Hastings, New Plymouth, Palmerston North, Wellington, Nelson, Christchurch, Dunedin, and Invercargill.
The retail outlet selection is reviewed every six years. Between reviews, retail outlets can be added to be surveyed to replace those that have closed or for other reasons.
- 2,400 other businesses and landlords that supply goods and services to households may be selected throughout the country. The selection of businesses is periodically reviewed.
Thus, if your business supplies goods and services, or if you are a landlord with rental properties, you may be selected to participate.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the consumer price surveys.
Retail outlets are visited weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annually by price collectors. Price collectors report to retail staff when visiting stores, then locate specific products, and collect prices. They may request assistance from staff when surveyed products are no longer stocked and they need guidance on suitable replacement products.
Other businesses and landlords are sent postal questionnaires monthly, quarterly, or annually.
We collect some information electronically, and in other cases use administrative collections (eg retail transaction data or ‘scanner data’).

## How to complete the survey
You fill in a paper questionnaire.
There's no questionnaire for retail outlets. Prices of goods in retail outlets are collected by field staff using handheld tablets.
- Consumers price index
- Retail and wholesale trade
- Contact us for survey help 26 May 2020
- Business survey information 31 July 2017
- Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data 20 May 2026
- Consumers price index 11 October 2017 Image: retail coat hanger
